mechanism_narrative: A declining or persistently low FEDFUNDS level relative to its 6-month trailing average signals monetary accommodation, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following low-rate periods are not statistically higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals accelerating labor market deterioration consistent with recessionary conditions, which depresses risk appetite and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, forward 60-day SPX returns are positive despite the rising-unemployment trigger, or the effect size is not statistically distinct from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: After a prolonged collapse in housing starts (HOUST), a flattening or modest upturn in HOUST from cyclical lows signals stabilization of the demand shock underlying the recession, foreshadowing an equity market bottoming process and positive forward returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at multi-decade low as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: HOUST stabilization episodes are followed by negative or flat 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical instances since 1970, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows restricted to trough regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the year-over-year rate of decline of INDPRO (i.e., industrial production contraction easing rather than worsening) signals an inflection in the supply-side contraction, which precedes equity market recoveries over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized decline rate improves (becomes less negative) for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO deceleration signals show no statistically significant difference from baseline returns (t-test p > 0.10) across at least two historical recession episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly PAYEMS job losses are moderating (smaller negative change) while FEDFUNDS remains near its cycle low, the joint signal of labor market stabilization plus continued monetary accommodation produces a reinforcing positive impulse to equity forward returns that neither series alone would generate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly decline shrinks for 2 consecutive months AND FEDFUNDS below 1%
regime_hint: monetary easing combined with labor market bottoming
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show forward 20-day returns statistically indistinguishable from single-factor (PAYEMS-only or FEDFUNDS-only) trigger episodes, indicating no true interaction effect (interaction coefficient p > 0.10 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in RSAFS (retail sales) reflects near-term consumer demand strength that carries directly into corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month change and forward 5-day SPX return is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above 4%, markets price in future Federal Reserve tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during high-inflation trigger episodes are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows restricted to high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in real GDP (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter decline) and a rising UNRATE indicates a deepening, self-reinforcing recession that outweighs any partial recovery signals, producing negative equity forward returns over the subsequent quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 0% AND UNRATE rising for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint GDPC1-contraction/UNRATE-rise trigger produces forward 60-day returns not significantly different from single-indicator triggers, or fails to show negative mean returns in at least 60% of historical recession quarters since 1960.
